LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO WATSONVILLE COAST PRODUCE INC'S CROSS COMPLAINT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST PLEAD FACTS AS TO THE BASIS FOR A DUTY. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FROM TODAY'S DATE. FURTHER, THE COURT INDICATED THAT THE 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THAT WAS GRANTED AS TO THE DEMURRER OF CROSS-DEFENDANT INTERWEST INSURANCE SERVICES HEARD ON JULY 27, 2011, STARTS TO RUN AS OF THIS DATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

PARAGRAPHS 26 THROUGH 28 OF THE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGE THAT THE BILLS WERE PROVIDED TO FIRST HEALTH ON DECEMBER 30 AND THE REPRICING NEEDED TO OCCUR BY DECEMBER 31. CROSS-COMPLAINANT DOES NOT ALLEGED OR SUGGESTED THAT IT CAN ALLEGE ANY ULTIMATE FACTS THAT FIRST HEALTH HAD A DUTY TO REPRICE WITHIN THIS SHORT TIME FRAME. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED WITH TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. CROSS-COMPLAINANT ADEQUATELY ALLEGES A DUTY BY THE REGENTS TO PROVIDE THE BILLINGS EARLIER THAN DECEMBER 30. SECTION 1300.71 DOES NOT BAR THE CLAIM AGAINST THE REGENTS. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT SHOW ON ITS FACE THAT THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS TIME-BARRED. JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSES THAT THE GENERAL COUNSEL FOR THE REGENTS IS A GOOD FRIEND OF HIS, BUT THAT RELATIONSHIP DOES NOT HAVE AN AFFECT ON JUDGE KAHN'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. PREVAILING PARTY TO E-MAIL ORDER TO THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Carol A. Treasure, Esq., from Cooper & Scully, 415-956-9400 x-125, for Cross defendant First Health Group Corp.
Russell G. Petti, Esq., Law Office, 818-952-2168, for Cross-Complainant and Opposing party Watsonville Coast Produce, Inc., appearing by telephone

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT FIRST HEALTH GROUP CORP.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend. Paragraphs 26 through 28 of the amended cross-complaint allege that the bills were provided to First Health on December 30 and the repricing needed to occur by December 31. Cross-complainant does not alleged or suggested that it can allege any ultimate facts that First Health had a duty to reprice within this short time frame. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher C. Saldana, from Shewry & Van Dyke, 619-233-8824, for Cross-Defendant and moving party InterWest Insurance Services, Inc., appearing by telephone
Russell G. Petti, Esq., Law Office, 818-952-2168, for Cross-Complainant and Opposing party Watsonville Coast Produce, Inc., appearing by telephone

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT INTERWEST INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled with ten days to answer. Cross-complainant sufficiently alleges that Interwest held itself out as an expert.
Prevaling party to e-mail order to the court. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher J. Hapak, Esq., from Stephenson Acquisto & Colman, 818-562-1253, for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant and moving party The Regents of the University of California
Russell G. Petti, Esq., Law Office, 818-952-2168, for Cross-Complainant and Opposing party Watsonville Coast Produce, Inc., appearing by telephone

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled with ten days to answer. Cross-complainant adequately alleges a duty by the Regents to provide the billings earlier than December 30. Section 1300.71 does not bar the claim against the Regents. The cross-complaint does not show on its face that the fourth cause of action is time-barred. Judge Kahn discloses that the general counsel for the Regents is a good friend of his, but that relationship does not have an affect on Judge Kahn's ability to be fair and impartial. Prevailing party to e-mail order to the court. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO WATSONVILLE COAST PRODUCE INC'S CROSS COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Christopher Hapak of Stephenson Acquisto & Colman (818.559.4477) for cross defendant Regents of the University of California; Carol A. Treasure of Cooper & Scully (956.9700 x125) for cross defendant First Health Group Corp.; Christopher Saldana of Shewry & Van Dyke (619.233.8824) appearing via court call for cross defendant Interwest Insurance Services, Inc.; Russell G. Petti of the Law Office of Russell G. Petti (818.952.2168) appearing via court call for cross complainant Watsonville Coast Produce, Inc.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: cross-defendant's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Cross-complainant must plead facts as to the basis for a duty. Cross-complainant has 20 days leave to amend from today's date. Further, the court indicated that the 20 days leave to amend that was granted as to the demurrer of cross-defendant Interwest Insurance Services heard on July 27, 2011, starts to run as of this date. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT HELD ITSELF OUT AS AN EXPERT. In addition, the Court stated cross-complainant has 20 days leave to amend. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Rhonda Aquilina, CSR 956
